name: Malpasillo Bridge and Pusa Narrows Loop
name_original: Santa Ana de Pusa – running circular por el puente de Malpasillo y la estrechura del Pusa
description: This easy 2.8-mile (4.6 km) jogging route in Santa Ana de Pusa features the historic Malpasillo Bridge and the scenic Pusa River narrowing.
region_intro: Jogging around La Jara offers diverse routes through a landscape characterized by river valleys, natural parks, and gentle hills. The region features trails along waterways like the Tajo, passes through areas with distinctive rock formations such as the Pusa Narrows, and includes sections within the Parque Nacional de Cabañeros. Runners can explore a mix of open terrain and forested paths, providing varied experiences for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around La Jara The most popular jogging route is Malpasillo Bridge and Pusa Narrows Loop, an easy 2.8 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 30 minutes to complete. This circular route offers views of the Malpasillo Bridge and the Pusa Narrows. Another top favourite among local runners is Camino Natural del Tajo – running circular from Aldeanueva de Barbarroya to the puente Amador, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) path. This route follows the Camino Natural del Tajo, offering riverside scenery. Local runners also love the Chorro de los Navalucillos Trail, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 58 minutes. Jogging around La Jara is defined by river paths, natural park landscapes, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes to more challenging running trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore La Jara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in La Jara?
A: La Jara offers a selection of 13 diverse running routes, catering to various fitness levels. These include 3 easy, 5 moderate, and 5 difficult trails, ensuring options for every runner.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in La Jara?
A: Yes, La Jara has several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189426823/ruta-de-la-encina-parque-nacional-de-cabaneros-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Encina — Parque Nacional de Cabañeros — round trip&lt;/a&gt;, which is 1.3 miles (2.1 km) long and takes about 16 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in La Jara?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, La Jara features several difficult running trails. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo – Chorrera Chica Waterfall loop from Parque Nacional de Cabañeros&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 11.1 miles (17.9 km) route with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in La Jara?
A: Many of the running routes in La Jara are circular, offering convenient start and end points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021486783/malpasillo-bridge-and-pusa-narrows-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpasillo Bridge and Pusa Narrows Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.8 miles (4.6 km) circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021479911/camino-natural-del-tajo-running-circular-from-aldeanueva-de-barbarroya-to-the-puente-amador" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Tajo – running circular from Aldeanueva de Barbarroya to the puente Amador&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.3 miles (8.6 km) loop.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on running trails in La Jara?
A: Running trails in La Jara offer diverse and scenic landscapes. You'll encounter river valleys, such as along the Tajo, distinctive rock formations like the Pusa Narrows, and sections within the Parque Nacional de Cabañeros. The region provides a mix of open terrain and forested paths, often with gentle hills.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls in La Jara?
A: Yes, you can find running routes that lead to beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021433391/chorro-de-los-navalucillos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de los Navalucillos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) route that takes you towards the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3336126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de Los Navalucillos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo – Chorrera Chica Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;, which includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5633420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera Chica Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the running routes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can discover several landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021486783/malpasillo-bridge-and-pusa-narrows-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpasillo Bridge and Pusa Narrows Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the historic Malpasillo Bridge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021479911/camino-natural-del-tajo-running-circular-from-aldeanueva-de-barbarroya-to-the-puente-amador" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Tajo&lt;/a&gt; route passes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3851007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amador Bridge or Azután Viaduct&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5417642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Azután Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in summits, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature in the region.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in La Jara?
A: Yes, for runners looking for longer distances, La Jara offers several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo – Chorrera Chica Waterfall loop from Parque Nacional de Cabañeros&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 11.1 miles (17.9 km) trail, providing a good endurance challenge.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in La Jara?
A: The region's climate, characterized by warm summers and mild winters, makes spring and autumn ideal for running in La Jara. The temperatures are pleasant, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Early mornings or late afternoons are generally best during the hotter summer months.

Q: Are there any running routes within the Parque Nacional de Cabañeros?
A: Yes, some running routes venture into the Parque Nacional de Cabañeros. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189426823/ruta-de-la-encina-parque-nacional-de-cabaneros-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Encina — Parque Nacional de Cabañeros — round trip&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route within the park, offering a chance to experience its unique ecosystem.

Q: Are there any running routes near the Tajo River?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021479911/camino-natural-del-tajo-running-circular-from-aldeanueva-de-barbarroya-to-the-puente-amador" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Tajo – running circular from Aldeanueva de Barbarroya to the puente Amador&lt;/a&gt; follows the Camino Natural del Tajo, providing beautiful riverside scenery along this significant waterway.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in La Jara?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running trails in La Jara. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from river paths to natural park landscapes and gentle hills, which provide diverse experiences for different fitness levels. The well-maintained paths and scenic views are frequently highlighted.
tips: Built in the 19th century to cross the course of the Pusa River, the bridge named Malpasillo is neoclassical in style and stands out in the picturesque landscape that surrounds it. It owes its name to the small and narrow rocky gorge where it sits, since it may seem like a jump but it is undoubtedly a "bad step" due to the danger it represents.

This area of the Pusa river known as the "Narrowness" has been used in the past with mills that, although they are currently in ruins, I recommend visiting them since the landscape is very picturesque.

faqs.question: Is the Santa Ana de Pusa running circular trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered easy and is suitable for all fitness levels. It's described as an 'easy walk' on easily accessible paths, making it a great option for those new to trail running or looking for a relaxed jog.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this running route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 4.5 kilometers long and generally takes around 30-35 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. If you prefer a leisurely pace, it could take up to an hour or more.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Santa Ana de Pusa running trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Santa Ana de Pusa is a small town, and you can typically find parking within the town itself to access the start of the circular route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of easily accessible paths. You'll experience rugged landscapes with granite formations, holm oaks, and various bushes. The section through the Estrechura of the Pusa River is a small, narrow rocky gorge.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlights include the picturesque Malpasillo Bridge, a neoclassical structure from the 19th century with three arches, and the scenic Estrechura of the Pusa River, a narrow rocky gorge where you can spot old, ruined mills.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near the river, to ensure their safety and respect for the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers extraordinary scenic beauty year-round. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer mornings or late afternoons are also good to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy this running route around Santa Ana de Pusa.

faqs.question: Are there any unique historical features to look out for?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Keep an eye out for the neoclassical Malpasillo Bridge, which dates back to 1852. Also, as you pass through the Estrechura of the Pusa River, you'll see the picturesque ruins of old mills along the riverbanks, offering a glimpse into the area's past.

faqs.question: Does this running route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Puente de Malpasillo, Camino de la zarza, Paseo del Prado, and Camino del Andariego.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Pusa river?
faqs.answer: You can expect extraordinary scenic beauty along the Pusa river. The river flows through wild landscapes characterized by granite formations, ancient holm oaks, and diverse scrubland, creating a very natural and picturesque setting.
